refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication packet loss at or below the customer-defined threshold (acceptance criterion: ≤1% under agreed network conditions) to ensure safe and effective remote equipment operation.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-009 addresses a critical quality-of-service requirement for teleoperation. Packet loss directly impacts command latency, feedback fidelity, and operator situational awareness—all safety-critical factors in remote heavy equipment control. Refinement clarifies that the requirement is conditional on 'agreed network conditions' (a boundary condition) and ties acceptance to a measurable, customer-negotiated metric rather than a fixed absolute.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct end-to-end packet loss measurement under representative network conditions (latency, jitter, bandwidth constraints) agreed with customer. Measure across multiple teleoperation sessions and load profiles. Verify that packet loss remains ≤1% (or customer target) for both command uplink (operator→equipment) and telemetry downlink (equipment→operator).
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-009.1: The teleoperation system shall detect and report packet loss events in real-time.
FR-NGHE-009.2: The teleoperation system shall implement error correction or retransmission mechanisms to minimize effective packet loss impact on command execution.
FR-NGHE-009.3: The teleoperation system shall gracefully degrade or alert the operator if packet loss exceeds the customer-defined threshold.
FR-NGHE-009.4: The teleoperation network link shall be provisioned and monitored to maintain packet loss ≤1% under agreed conditions.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement adaptive forward error correction (FEC) and selective retransmission in the teleoperation protocol stack, with real-time packet loss monitoring and operator alerting.
DP-B: Provision redundant network paths (dual connectivity) with automatic failover and load balancing to reduce single-path packet loss impact.
DP-C: Deploy a hybrid approach combining FEC, selective retransmission, redundant network paths, and real-time packet loss telemetry with operator dashboard feedback.
DP-D: Rely on underlying network service-level agreements (SLAs) and assume the network provider guarantees ≤1% packet loss; implement only basic packet loss monitoring.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Deploy a hybrid approach combining forward error correction (FEC) and selective retransmission in the teleoperation protocol, redundant network paths with automatic failover, real-time packet loss telemetry collection, and operator-facing alerts/dashboard feedback to maintain effective packet loss ≤1% under agreed network conditions.

potential_sources: DP-C couples teleoperation protocol design (FEC/retransmission logic) with network infrastructure provisioning (redundancy, failover). This is acceptable because both are necessary to meet the FR and are logically interdependent.
DP-C also couples operator interface (alerting/dashboard) with backend telemetry collection. This is a standard and acceptable coupling in safety-critical systems.
No evidence of circular or contradictory dependencies detected.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is more complex than DP-A or DP-D but is justified by high safety criticality and the need for defense-in-depth. DP-B alone (redundancy without protocol-level mitigation) would be insufficient if both paths experience correlated packet loss. DP-D is rejected due to high safety criticality and lack of direct control over network provider performance.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Customer-defined packet loss threshold and agreed network conditions will be formally documented before detailed design.
Assumption: Redundant network paths (e.g., cellular + satellite, or dual ISP) are available at the deployment site.
Risk: FEC overhead may increase bandwidth consumption; must be balanced against available network capacity.
Risk: Failover latency during path switching could momentarily exceed operator tolerance; failover logic must be tuned and tested.
Risk: Operator alerting/dashboard adds UI complexity; must be designed to avoid operator distraction or alert fatigue.
Risk: Real-time telemetry collection and analysis add computational load; must be validated on target hardware.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is sound. Recommend that detailed design phase explicitly define: (1) FEC algorithm and parameters (e.g., Reed-Solomon, Turbo codes), (2) retransmission timeout and retry limits, (3) redundant network path selection criteria and failover thresholds, (4) packet loss telemetry granularity (per-session, per-command, rolling window), and (5) operator alert thresholds and UI design.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: The Teleoperation module owns the end-to-end teleoperation communication stack, including protocol logic (FEC, retransmission), packet loss monitoring, operator alerting, and integration with redundant network paths. It interfaces with the Vehicle module (for command execution and telemetry generation), Safety Services (for safety-critical alerts), and the Network Link (for physical/logical connectivity).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands and tasking requests from the tele-operator console; Teleoperation module decodes, applies FEC/retransmission logic, and forwards to Vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits vehicle state, sensor data, and packet loss metrics back to the tele-operator console; includes real-time packet loss telemetry for operator dashboard.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety-critical alerts from Safety Services (e.g., emergency stop); also sends packet loss degradation alerts to Safety Services if threshold is exceeded.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interfaces with redundant network paths (primary and backup); Teleoperation module monitors link quality and triggers failover logic.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system container. Assigning DP-C to NGHE Solution would diffuse teleoperation protocol logic, network redundancy, and monitoring across multiple sub-modules without clear ownership. Not recommended as the primary IM boundary.

Vehicle module is responsible for equipment control and onboard telemetry generation. Teleoperation protocol logic (FEC, retransmission, failover) is not a vehicle concern; it belongs in the communication layer. Assigning DP-C to Vehicle would create inappropriate coupling and diffusion.

Teleoperation module is the natural and cohesive home for DP-C. It owns the protocol stack, packet loss monitoring, operator alerting, and network path management. All sub-components of DP-C (FEC, retransmission, redundancy logic, telemetry collection, operator interface) are contained within this module's scope. Minimal diffusion risk.

Safety Services is responsible for safety-critical decision-making and alerts. While packet loss degradation is a safety concern, the implementation of FEC, retransmission, and network redundancy is not a safety service function. Safety Services should consume packet loss telemetry from Teleoperation and make safety decisions (e.g., trigger emergency stop if packet loss is unacceptable), but should not own the protocol logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the optimal IM boundary for DP-C because: (1) it owns the teleoperation protocol and communication stack by definition, (2) FEC and retransmission are protocol-level concerns, (3) network path redundancy and failover are communication infrastructure concerns, (4) packet loss monitoring and telemetry are integral to teleoperation quality-of-service, and (5) operator alerting is a teleoperation UI concern. All sub-components of DP-C are logically and functionally contained within Teleoperation's scope.
containment_risks: Risk: Teleoperation module may become complex if it also owns the tele-operator console UI. Recommend clear separation between protocol/network logic (Teleoperation backend) and operator interface (Teleoperation frontend or separate UI module).

Risk: Redundant network path provisioning may require coordination with external network providers or infrastructure teams. Teleoperation module must define clear interfaces and contracts for network path availability.

Risk: Real-time packet loss telemetry and failover logic add computational and memory overhead. Must validate that Teleoperation module can execute on target hardware (e.g., edge gateway, vehicle onboard computer) without performance degradation.
Risk: Safety Services must be able to consume packet loss telemetry and make safety decisions independently. Ensure SafetyAlert interface is well-defined and does not create circular dependencies.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign DP-C to Teleoperation module. Recommend further decomposition of Teleoperation into sub-modules: (1) Protocol Stack (FEC, retransmission, packet loss detection), (2) Network Redundancy Manager (failover logic, path selection), (3) Telemetry Collector (packet loss metrics, real-time monitoring), and (4) Operator Interface (alerts, dashboard). This sub-decomposition will be addressed in the next level of functional decomposition.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C directly and comprehensively addresses FR-NGHE-009. The refined FR specifies that packet loss must be ≤1% under agreed conditions. DP-C implements FEC, retransmission, redundant paths, and monitoring—all necessary mechanisms to achieve this target. The DP is neither over-specified (e.g., does not mandate specific algorithms) nor under-specified (e.g., does not assume network SLAs alone). Coupling is acceptable and justified by safety criticality.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk: Teleoperation module is the natural home for DP-C. No evidence of logic scattered across multiple modules.

Moderate integration risk: Teleoperation must interface cleanly with Vehicle (for command/telemetry), Safety Services (for alerts), and Network Link (for redundancy). These interfaces are well-defined and non-circular.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module achieves 0.95 containment score for DP-C. All protocol logic, network redundancy, monitoring, and operator alerting are contained within this module. Ownership is clear. Recommended sub-decomposition into Protocol Stack, Network Redundancy Manager, Telemetry Collector, and Operator Interface will further improve modularity in the next level.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-009 is feasible. Packet loss can be measured directly via telemetry collection and network monitoring. Acceptance criteria (≤1% under agreed conditions) is quantitative and testable. Verification plan should include: (1) lab testing with simulated packet loss, (2) field testing under representative network conditions, (3) long-duration soak tests to validate failover and recovery.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear. Teleoperation module is responsible for implementing and maintaining packet loss ≤1%. Vehicle module is responsible for executing commands reliably once received. Safety Services is responsible for monitoring packet loss telemetry and making safety decisions if threshold is exceeded. Network Link is responsible for providing redundant paths. Clear ownership and interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Formalize customer-defined packet loss threshold and agreed network conditions in a requirements specification document before detailed design.
Define detailed acceptance test plan for FR-NGHE-009, including test scenarios, network conditions, measurement methodology, and pass/fail criteria.
Conduct design review of DP-C to finalize FEC algorithm, retransmission parameters, failover thresholds, and telemetry granularity.
Plan sub-decomposition of Teleoperation module in next level to address Protocol Stack, Network Redundancy Manager, Telemetry Collector, and Operator Interface as separate sub-modules.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-009 is not atomic and has been refined into four sub-FRs (packet loss detection, error correction, graceful degradation, network provisioning). DP-C is sound and well-justified. Teleoperation module is the correct IM boundary with high containment (0.95) and low diffusion risk. All gates pass. The next level of functional decomposition should address the four sub-FRs and further decompose the Teleoperation module into Protocol Stack, Network Redundancy Manager, Telemetry Collector, and Operator Interface sub-modules. This will provide greater clarity on implementation details and ownership boundaries.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-009.1: The teleoperation system shall detect and report packet loss events in real-time.
FR-NGHE-009.2: The teleoperation system shall implement error correction or retransmission mechanisms to minimize effective packet loss impact on command execution.
FR-NGHE-009.3: The teleoperation system shall gracefully degrade or alert the operator if packet loss exceeds the customer-defined threshold.
FR-NGHE-009.4: The teleoperation network link shall be provisioned and monitored to maintain packet loss ≤1% under agreed conditions.

assumptions: Customer-defined packet loss threshold and agreed network conditions will be formally documented before detailed design.
Redundant network paths (e.g., cellular + satellite, or dual ISP) are available or can be provisioned at deployment sites.
Teleoperation module will be further decomposed into sub-modules (Protocol Stack, Network Redundancy Manager, Telemetry Collector, Operator Interface) in the next level.
Safety Services module will consume packet loss telemetry from Teleoperation and make independent safety decisions.
Vehicle module will execute commands reliably once received from Teleoperation, regardless of network conditions.
open_risks: FEC overhead may increase bandwidth consumption; must be balanced against available network capacity during detailed design.
Failover latency during redundant path switching could momentarily exceed operator tolerance; failover logic must be tuned and tested.
Operator alerting/dashboard adds UI complexity; must be designed to avoid operator distraction or alert fatigue.
Real-time telemetry collection and analysis add computational load; must be validated on target hardware.
Redundant network path provisioning may require coordination with external network providers; clear contracts and SLAs must be established.
evidence_needed: Formal customer requirements document specifying packet loss threshold and agreed network conditions.
Network topology and redundancy architecture diagram showing primary and backup paths.
FEC algorithm selection and parameter justification (e.g., Reed-Solomon vs. Turbo codes, code rate, latency impact).
Retransmission timeout and retry limit analysis based on network latency and operator tolerance.
Failover logic design and simulation results showing failover latency and recovery time.
Operator interface mockup and usability study results for packet loss alerts and dashboard.
Hardware performance analysis showing computational and memory overhead of real-time telemetry collection.
Test plan and acceptance criteria for FR-NGHE-009 verification.
